name: The audit policy (SACL) on an object was changed
definition: Windows Security Event ID 4715 records The audit policy (SACL) on an object was changed.
trigger_scenarios: Windows logs the event when the audited security operation occurs.

key_fields.explanation: Object Name and SACL change removing Success or Failure auditing from a sensitive file or registry key is audit suppression; compare previous and new SDDL.
The initiating account and Logon ID support attribution and correlation to 4624 and 4688.
The affected host determines the operational impact.

false_positives: Documented maintenance, policy changes, or approved administration can generate this event.
Validate change control and host role before suppressing.

attck_mapping.technique_name: Impair Defenses: Disable Windows Event Logging

detection_notes: Object Name and SACL change removing Success or Failure auditing from a sensitive file or registry key is audit suppression; compare previous and new SDDL. This is a T1562.002 Impair Defenses: Disable Windows Event Logging signal; preserve the concrete value and correlate the actor, process, and related events. Review the change against the approved baseline in a window=60 minute correlation period.
kql_snippet:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4715
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account
